summary: Phase 6.A.1 (drift-detection sweep before Phase 6.A.2's inventory + equipment + atlas-extension implementation) complete. The sweep landed exactly the items pinned in docs/PHASES.md Phase 6 callout block: (1) Phase 3.A.2 carry-forward — applyFloorEntry redundant param dropped at src/sim/run.ts:160-180 (signature reduced from (state, newFloor, newFloorState) to (state, newFloorState); reads newFloorState.floor.entrance.{y,x} since the FloorState invariant pins floorState.floor as the floor those entities live on); 3 call sites updated (src/main.ts:580, src/sim/harness.ts:99, tests/sim/run.test.ts:217); SIM_DIGEST golden d212f5cf… (Phase 3 frozen) unchanged confirming byte-equivalent refactor. (2) Phase 3.A.2 carry-forward — ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES: Uint8Array shared constant exported from src/sim/params.ts (single source-of-truth for the encoded roll-domain anchor; src/sim/combat.ts imports it instead of re-encoding via utf8(ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_TEXT); the byte-explicit pre-image test in tests/sim/combat.test.ts deliberately keeps the manual utf8() re-encoding for audit-hostile-reader purposes per the original carry-forward); uses utf8(...) helper from src/core/hash to match the established style (matches ATLAS_SEED_DOMAIN_BYTES pattern in src/atlas/seed.ts); not Object.freeze-wrapped because Object.freeze rejects array-buffer views with elements. (3) Phase 3.A.2 carry-forward — tick player-action resolution in src/sim/turn.ts refactored from if/else if chain to switch/case on action.type with explicit cases for ACTION_TYPE_WAIT/MOVE/ATTACK/DESCEND and a default no-op preserving the Phase 1 frozen 'additive vocabulary' contract; gives Phase 6.A.2 developers a clear place to add pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use cases and surface no-op-on-typo bugs during dev-loop testing rather than at integration time; SIM_DIGEST golden unchanged confirming byte-equivalent refactor. (4) Phase 5.A.2 carry-forward N1 — fillStyle = '#000' inline literal in src/render/canvas.ts:150 extracted to module-top CANVAS_BACKGROUND_COLOR named constant alongside the other slot/tile constants (placement fixed per code-review review of the initial draft to avoid sandwiching between spritePixelCoord's docstring and body). (5) docs/ARCHITECTURE.md — appended a new 'Phase 6 frozen contracts (items + currency + equipment)' section between 'Phase 5 frozen contracts' and 'Build-time constants' (~155 new lines) covering: inventory data shape with deterministic ordering (sorted by kind ASC, count DESC; capacity unbounded in Phase 6); equipment slot enumeration (weapon, cyberware; frozen for Phase 6); 5 additive action types pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use reusing existing TAG_ITEM=0x20 wire tag (no ACTION_VERSION bump); item-effect resolution path via existing rollBytes subhash with 3 new domain anchors (item:effect:heal, item:effect:atk-bonus, item:effect:def-bonus); equipment-modifier injection at combat time as integer addition to existing combat:atk-bonus / combat:counter-bonus rolls; registry append-only invariant with the long-deferred Phase 2 decision-memo decision 6 immutability test landing in 6.A.2 as the second writer adds entries; atlas extension coordinate-stable per addendum 3a (Phase 4 sprite coordinates preserved); ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values bumped in 6.A.2; inventory-from-log reconstruction invariant; deferred Phase 6 contracts (capacity bound, stack/unstack, rarity tiers, flavor text). Code-reviewer verdict APPROVE with 0 blockers (artifacts/code-review-phase-6-A-1.md). All four cosmetic review nits addressed in this same approval pass: (a) Phase 3 item count corrected from 'single entry' to '5 entries' (cred-chip + cyberdeck-mod-1 + stim-patch + trauma-pack + weapon.knife); (b) registry-immutability test claim reworded from 'lands in 6.A.1' to 'lands in 6.A.2 as the second writer adds the new entries' (matches the original Phase 2 decision-memo deferral); (c) ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES uses utf8(...) helper from core/hash matching the seed.ts style; (d) CANVAS_BACKGROUND_COLOR moved to the module-top constants block. All gates green: 865 tests passing across 58 test files (no net-new tests; refactor-only sweep); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build 69.18 KB raw / 26.06 KB gzipped (was 69.17 / 26.03 at HEAD of phase-5.B; +0.01 KB raw / +0.03 KB gzipped delta from minor minified differences in the switch/case refactor + import changes; well under the 75 KB budget; ~67% headroom). No net-new inventory / equipment / item-recipe code per the Phase 6.A.1 acceptance criterion.
suggested_commit_message: phase-6.A.1: drift sweep (Phase 3.A.2 carry-forwards landed + Phase 5.A.2 N1 + Phase 6 ARCHITECTURE.md frozen contracts)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm Phase 5.B foundation (HEAD of phase-5.B commit 542e36d) had not drifted before Phase 6.A.1 implementation began, and confirm the 6.A.1 changes maintain the foundation's invariants. Phase 5 was end-to-end verified by phase-5.B (CI run 25328387560 green: deploy + 54 cross-runtime Playwright assertions + cross-OS atlas matrix).
verification_sprint.unit_tests: Pre-phase: 865 passed across 58 test files at HEAD of phase-5.B. Post-phase: 865 passed across 58 test files. Net-new: +0 tests — Phase 6.A.1 is a refactor-only sweep + a doc addition; no behavioral changes that would warrant new tests. The byte-equivalence of the applyFloorEntry param refactor and the tick switch/case refactor is asserted via the existing SIM_DIGEST cross-runtime self-test (the golden hex remains 321c09e5f87e879aebdf58ccaaada5e85f8a114bf01f4e012039eced5dba079e — any subtle behavior change would surface there immediately).
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The new ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES export + import update + the switch/case refactor + the named CANVAS_BACKGROUND_COLOR constant all comply with the existing src/sim/** + src/render/** lint scopes (no float arithmetic, no JSON.parse, no member-access bans).
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors (TS strict + exactOptionalPropertyTypes + noUncheckedIndexedAccess). The applyFloorEntry signature change propagates correctly to all 3 call sites; no leftover `newFloor` references.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green; dist/assets/index-*.js = 69.18 KB raw / 26.06 KB gzipped (was 69.17 KB / 26.03 KB at HEAD of phase-5.B; +0.01 KB raw / +0.03 KB gzipped delta from minor minified differences in the switch/case refactor + import changes; the named constant is inlined by the minifier so no bundle delta from that). Well under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget; ~67% headroom.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: No user-visible changes in 6.A.1. The live diagnostic + playable surfaces continue to render exactly as approved at the HEAD of phase-5.B; the cross-runtime Playwright suite (54 assertions) continues to pass; the cross-OS atlas matrix continues to assert byte-identical assets/atlas.png across ubuntu-latest / macos-latest / windows-latest (the atlas binary is unchanged in 6.A.1; recipe additions land in 6.A.2).

phase_3_a_2_carry_forward_apply_floor_en.evidence: src/sim/run.ts:160-180 — applyFloorEntry signature reduced from (state, newFloor, newFloorState) to (state, newFloorState); reads newFloorState.floor.entrance.{y,x}. The FloorState invariant ensures floorState.floor === floor at construction time (src/sim/run.ts:124-128 set FloorState.floor from spawnFloorEntities's input parameter), so byte-equivalent. 3 call sites updated: src/main.ts:580, src/sim/harness.ts:99, tests/sim/run.test.ts:217. SIM_DIGEST cross-runtime self-test (321c09e5f87e879aebdf58ccaaada5e85f8a114bf01f4e012039eced5dba079e) unchanged confirming byte-equivalence.

phase_3_a_2_carry_forward_roll_domain_an.evidence: src/sim/params.ts exports ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES: Uint8Array using utf8(ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_TEXT) from src/core/hash (matches the ATLAS_SEED_DOMAIN_BYTES style in src/atlas/seed.ts). src/sim/combat.ts:27-32 imports ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES instead of re-encoding via utf8(ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_TEXT); the local `const ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR` is removed. The byte-explicit pre-image test in tests/sim/combat.test.ts:37 still uses utf8(ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_TEXT) deliberately — that test reconstructs the pre-image from scratch as audit proof, and sharing the bytes constant would defeat the audit-hostile-reader purpose (documented in the original Phase 3.A.2 review N3). Not Object.freeze-wrapped because Object.freeze rejects array-buffer views with elements; documented inline.

phase_3_a_2_carry_forward_tick_switch_ca.evidence: src/sim/turn.ts:112-181 — tick player-action resolution refactored from if/else if chain to switch/case on action.type with explicit cases for ACTION_TYPE_WAIT/MOVE/ATTACK/DESCEND and a default no-op preserving the Phase 1 frozen 'additive vocabulary' contract. The default branch's docstring explains the contract preservation. The switch/case structure makes Phase 6.A.2's pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use additions a localized change. SIM_DIGEST golden unchanged confirming runtime byte-equivalence.

phase_5_a_2_carry_forward_canvas_backgro.evidence: src/render/canvas.ts:88-96 — extracted the inline `fillStyle = '#000'` literal in drawScene to a module-top CANVAS_BACKGROUND_COLOR named constant alongside the other slot/tile constants (placement corrected from initial draft per code-review feedback). docstring justifies the choice (matches cyberpunk-neon-v1 palette transparent slot's RGB). Behavior unchanged.

no_net_new_inventory_equipment_recipe_co.evidence: git status --short shows zero new files under src/sim/ (no inventory.ts, no equipment.ts), zero new files under src/atlas/recipes/, zero new files in src/registries/. The only working-tree files modified are: artifacts/phase-update.json (deleted by host wrapper between sessions), docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, src/main.ts, src/render/canvas.ts, src/sim/combat.ts, src/sim/harness.ts, src/sim/params.ts, src/sim/run.ts, src/sim/turn.ts, tests/sim/run.test.ts. All scope-correct drift-sweep deliverables; no inventory mechanics, no item recipes, no UI screens land in 6.A.1.

architecture_md_phase_6_section.evidence: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md gains a 'Phase 6 frozen contracts (items + currency + equipment)' section between 'Phase 5 frozen contracts' and 'Build-time constants' (~155 lines). Coverage: inventory data shape (Player gains readonly inventory + equipment fields; sorted by kind ASC, count DESC; capacity unbounded); equipment slot enumeration (weapon, cyberware; frozen for Phase 6 — bumping requires architecture-red-team review); 5 additive action types (pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use; reuse TAG_ITEM=0x20 wire tag, no ACTION_VERSION bump per Phase 1 frozen contract); item-effect resolution path through existing rollBytes subhash (3 new domains: item:effect:heal, item:effect:atk-bonus, item:effect:def-bonus); equipment-modifier injection (integer addition to existing combat:atk-bonus / combat:counter-bonus); registry append-only invariant with corrected Phase 3 baseline (5 entries: cred-chip, cyberdeck-mod-1, stim-patch, trauma-pack, weapon.knife) and the corrected immutability-test deferral (lands in 6.A.2 alongside the second writer); atlas extension coordinate-stability (Phase 4 sprite coordinates preserved); ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values bumped in 6.A.2; inventory-from-log reconstruction invariant; deferred Phase 6 contracts.

code_review.evidence: artifacts/code-review-phase-6-A-1.md: verdict APPROVE. 0 blockers. All 7 verification points pass. Four cosmetic suggestions: (1) ARCHITECTURE.md falsely claimed registry-immutability test 'lands in 6.A.1' — addressed by rewording to 'lands in 6.A.2 as the second writer adds the new entries' (matches original Phase 2 decision-memo decision 6 deferral); (2) Phase 3 ships 5 items not 1 — addressed by enumerating the 5 entries explicitly; (3) ROLL_DOMAIN_ANCHOR_BYTES used new TextEncoder().encode(...) instead of the established utf8(...) helper — addressed by switching to utf8(...) for style consistency with src/atlas/seed.ts:14; (4) CANVAS_BACKGROUND_COLOR sandwiched between spritePixelCoord's docstring and body — addressed by moving to the module-top constants block. All four nits addressed in this same approval pass; final gates re-verified post-fixup.

follow_ups_carried_forward_to_phase_6_a_: Implementation per docs/PHASES.md:426-431: inventory + equipment types in src/sim/types.ts (Player gains readonly inventory + equipment fields per the ARCHITECTURE.md Phase 6 spec); 5 new Action descriptors in src/core/encode.ts additive to the Phase 1 schema (pickup/drop/equip/unequip/use; reuse TAG_ITEM=0x20 wire tag); tick() extended in src/sim/turn.ts to handle the 5 new actions with deterministic effect resolution (the Phase 6.A.1 switch/case refactor makes this a localized addition of 5 new cases); item registry expanded in src/registries/items.ts from 5 to ~20 entries across weapons / cyberware / consumables / currency categories; ~13 new atlas recipes added under src/atlas/recipes/ (registry append-only; existing 7 sprite coordinates preserved); assets/atlas.png regenerated → ATLAS_DIGEST golden bumped + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values bumped; new SIM_DIGEST extension via a SELF_TEST_LOG that exercises the inventory loop OR a new INVENTORY_DIGEST golden; inventory + equipment UI screens in new src/ui/inventory.ts + src/ui/equipment.ts (read-only on RunState per Phase 5 contract); inventory-from-log reconstruction test asserting replay(actions) === RunState byte-equality; the long-deferred Phase 2 decision-memo decision 6 registry-immutability test lands as the second writer adds entries.

Phase 5.A.2 carry-forwards still held back: Direction cast helper in src/input/keyboard.ts (defer until Phase 6.A.2 actually adds inventory keys to DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS); tests/ui/fake-dom.ts happy-dom evaluation (defer until 6.A.2's inventory + equipment UI tests need DOM features fake-dom.ts doesn't provide; if so, migrate then).
next_phase: Phase 6.A.2 — sandbox-verifiable inventory + equipment + atlas extension implementation per docs/PHASES.md:426-431. Acceptance criteria: item effects deterministic and resolved through the same hash-driven combat path; inventory state fully reconstructible from the action log alone (asserted by a new test that runs a scripted action log, captures the final inventory, replays the same log from genesis, and asserts byte-identical inventory); CI fails if checked-in atlas is stale (existing git diff --exit-code -- assets/ gate); the diagnostic + playable surfaces continue to render so the cross-runtime Playwright suite from Phases 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B/5.B keeps passing alongside the new inventory UI and the bumped ATLAS_DIGEST + preset-seed expectedHash values; npm ci && npm run lint && npm run test && npm run build && npm run test:e2e all green inside the sandbox. Material design choices that surface during 6.A.2 (inventory shape edge cases, equipment slot rejection error messages, atlas-recipe naming for items) are resolved via the ARCHITECTURE.md Phase 6 frozen-contract spec or a brief strategy-planner pass with output as ARCHITECTURE.md amendments.
